sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of shared device fingerprints among flagged withdrawals.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified two major shared fingerprints ('a1b2c3d4e5f6...' and 'deadbeef...') used by multiple accounts across different countries (RUS, FRA).

sql_findings.query_summary: Analysis of transaction error codes for the flagged cluster.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found a high volume of 'card_restricted' errors, confirming carding/card testing behavior.

sql_findings.query_summary: IP history analysis for cross-account links.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed IP address '41.44.55.66' is shared by accounts that also share device fingerprints, solidifying the link between entities.

web_references.title: Real-Time Fraud Monitoring for Enterprise Fintech Stacks
web_references.relevance: Corroborates the pattern of using device fingerprinting and velocity to detect organized carding rings.

web_references.title: How machine learning powers real-time fraud detection in payments
web_references.relevance: Discusses how machine learning uses device profiles to identify clusters of fraudulent accounts.

title: Account Takeover (ATO) via Geo-Velocity Anomaly (Impossible Travel)
formal_pattern_name: Account Takeover (ATO) via Geo-Velocity Anomaly (Impossible Travel)

sql_findings.query_summary: Searched for shared IP or device infrastructure across different customers.
sql_findings.result_summary: Identified IP 41.44.55.66 being used by two distinct customer accounts (47f31044... and 80320da3...) within the past 7 days.
Withdrawal 6333209d... showed high risk scores for Recipient Analysis (0.83), Device Fingerprint (0.79), and Geographic Risk (0.65), confirming the multi-layered failure described in the prompt.

web_references.title: Impossible Travel 101: What You Need to Know - Ping Identity
web_references.relevance: Explains the 'Impossible Travel' detection technique and how it identifies unauthorized access.

web_references.title: What are risk detections? - Microsoft Entra ID Protection
web_references.relevance: Describes how Microsoft Entra ID uses 'Atypical Travel' and 'Impossible Travel' to detect credential compromise.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers with low trade counts and high withdrawal activity.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found multiple accounts (Petrov, Hassan) with only 1 trade each but 4-6 withdrawals totaling thousands of dollars.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check for shared payment methods among low-activity traders.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that at least two separate accounts with minimal trading activity shared a Visa card ending in '1111'.

web_references.title: FINRA: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Red Flags
web_references.relevance: Describes minimal trading as a key red flag for money laundering.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify accounts sharing device fingerprints and their geographic locations.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two major shared fingerprints linked to accounts in FRA, RUS, and EGY, indicating a cross-border fraud ring.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyze the time window between deposits and withdrawals for shared-device accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed a rapid 'churn' cycle where withdrawals occur within 16-24 hours of deposits, often for amounts significantly larger than the trading volume.

sql_findings.query_summary: Examine trading activity for suspected fraud ring members.
sql_findings.result_summary: Discovered 'token trading' behavior where users place trades as low as $10 while withdrawing thousands, a tactic used to bypass automated AML filters.

web_references.title: Common types of online payment fraud and how to prevent them
web_references.relevance: Describes card testing (carding) and geographic inconsistency as key fraud indicators.

web_references.title: TYPOLOGIES IN THE FINANCIAL SECTOR
web_references.relevance: Identifies rapid movement of funds and minimal initial activity as a money laundering/fraud typology.

title: Pass-through Money Laundering / Credit Card Cashing
formal_pattern_name: Pass-through Money Laundering / Credit Card Cashing

sql_findings.query_summary: Aggregated deposit, trade, and withdrawal activity for customers flagged with high 'trading_behavior' risk scores.
sql_findings.result_summary: Multiple customers (Fatima Nour, Sophie Laurent, Emma Davies) showed high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios with near-zero trade counts. Several used placeholder device fingerprints like 'deadbeefdeadbeef...'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of shared withdrawal recipient accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed a direct link between Ahmed Hassan and Fatima Nour, who both withdrew funds to the same Egyptian bank account (EG80...901) on the same day.

sql_findings.query_summary: Detailed transaction and withdrawal audit for Fatima Nour.
sql_findings.result_summary: The user deposited $1,500 but attempted withdrawals totaling over $18,000 to multiple recipients including 'John Doe' and 'Elena Volkov', indicating balance manipulation or float exploitation.

web_references.title: Money Laundering Red Flags (FFIEC)
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'Pass-through' and 'Layering' patterns where accounts are used briefly for large transfers with no business purpose.

web_references.title: Check Kiting in the Digital Age - NICE Actimize
web_references.relevance: Explains 'Check Kiting' and 'Float' fraud where more money is taken out than put in.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identify customers sharing device fingerprints and recipient bank accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found two customers (ahmed.hassan@example.com and fatima.nour@example.com) sharing device 'deadbeef...' and recipient account 'EG800002000156789012345678901'.

sql_findings.query_summary: Check IP address overlap and VPN usage for the identified accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Both accounts shared the same IP (41.44.55.66) in Egypt; no VPN was detected in the IP history for these specific sessions.

web_references.title: Block multi-accounting with Device Intelligence - SEON Docs
web_references.relevance: Describes the mechanics of multi-accounting and how device fingerprinting identifies fraud rings.

web_references.title: Fraud Ring: Definition, 6 Types, & How to Stop Criminals - Unit21
web_references.relevance: Defines fraud rings and explains how they reuse financial credentials and device IDs across multiple accounts.

sql_findings.query_summary: Identified accounts with shared device fingerprints and high withdrawal-to-deposit ratios.
sql_findings.result_summary: Found 4 accounts (Laurent, Petrov, Hassan, Nour) sharing two device fingerprints. Withdrawal requests were consistently >95% of the initial deposit amount.

sql_findings.query_summary: Analyzed transaction error rates and IP history for the identified accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: Account 'Sophie Laurent' showed a 75% card error rate (3 failures in 4 attempts), indicating card testing. Accounts 'Ahmed Hassan' and 'Fatima Nour' were linked to the same IP address in Egypt.

web_references.title: Fintech Fraud Detection: Money Laundering Patterns
web_references.relevance: Describes the 'pass-through' method of using financial platforms to launder stolen funds via rapid withdrawals.

web_references.title: Common types of financial fraud: Carding and Cash-outs
web_references.relevance: Explains how fraudsters use trading platforms to cash out stolen credit cards.
